title: Vietnam-India seminar boosts textile, leather ties
content:
The Vietnam Trade Office in India held an online seminar to boost textile, footwear and leather collaboration amid shifting global supply chains. 
Stakeholders highlighted complementary strengths, with Vietnam strong in garments and India in raw materials. 
Discussions focused on silk cooperation, investment opportunities and technology exchange. 
Upcoming trade fairs and exhibitions were also promoted.

title: ICE cotton rally reverses sharply on profit booking at higher levels
content:
Intercontinental Exchange cotton (ICE) futures reversed sharply after hitting near two-year highs, closing lower on profit booking despite supportive fundamentals.
Rising crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions underpin demand, while planting progress and higher stocks add pressure.
The decline is seen as a correction, with underlying bullish sentiment intact.

title: Vietnam’s Hai Phong waives port infra fees for petro shipments in 2026
content:
Vietnam’s port city of Hai Phong will waive port infrastructure fees for petrol, oil and gas shipments until December 31 under a new resolution adopted by the municipal People’s Council. 
It applies to organisations and individuals involved in the collection, payment, management and use of such fees. 
For other liquid goods not classified as petrol, oil or gas, existing fee levels remain unchanged.

title: Asos makes “steady progress” on topline in H1 as losses narrow
content: Asos has maintained its guidance for FY26 after demonstrating “steady progress” on topline in H1, despite Middle East disruptions.

title: Bangladesh, EU strike deal on trade, investment, sustainable development
content: The EU and Bangladesh have signed a new Partnership and Cooperation Agreement (PCA) to boost trade, investment and “sustainable development”.
